records.ety: [F. curie stable, for older escurie, escuirie (confused somewhat with F. cuyer, OF. escuyer, squire), LL. scuria, OHG. skiura, sc<?/ra, barn, shed, G. scheuer, from a root meaning to cover, protect, and akin to L. scutum shield. See Esquire, and cf. Ecurie, Querry.]

defs: A large stable or lodge for horses.
An officer of princes or nobles, charged with the care of their horses.
notes: ☞ In England equerries are officers of the royal household in the department of the Master of the Horse.
